title: DataFolders Object
description: Collection object the provides a list of data folders.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new folder within the parent folder.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Get the current list of all folders.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the folder specified using the ID of the folder.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the folder specified using the name of the folder.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of folders in this collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: DataFolders.add Method
add.description: Creates a new folder within the parent folder.
add.return_description: Returns the created DataFolder or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the folder. This must be unique with respect to the other folders within the parent folder.

asArray.title: DataFolders.asArray Method
asArray.description: Get the current list of all folders.
asArray.return_description: Returns the current list of all folders.

classType.title: DataFolders.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: DataFolders.item Method
item.return_description: Returns the item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the folder to return. The first folder in the list has an index of 0.

itemById.title: DataFolders.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the folder specified using the ID of the folder.
itemById.return_description: Returns the folder or null if a folder with the specified ID is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to return. This is the same ID used by the APS Data Management API.

itemByName.title: DataFolders.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the folder specified using the name of the folder.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the folder or null if a folder of the specified name is not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the folder to return.

count.title: DataFolders.count Property
count.description: The number of folders in this collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataFolders_var.count

isValid.title: DataFolders.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataFolders_var.isValid

objectType.title: DataFolders.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataFolders_var.objectType
